name: Mistral AI Agents API
what_it_does: Mistral AI's Agents API enables building multi-step AI agents that can call tools, execute code, and perform web search using Mistral's models. Built on Mistral's European cloud infrastructure — significant for EU data residency. Includes native code execution, web search, and function calling in an agent framework.
use_cases: EU-compliant agent workflows where data must stay in European cloud
Agents needing native code execution without additional sandbox setup
Multi-step reasoning with native web search capability
Function calling and tool use with Mistral's efficient models
Enterprise European AI deployments with clear data residency
not_for: Highest capability tasks (Mistral models are efficient but not frontier quality)
Complex multi-agent orchestration (limited multi-agent coordination features)
Teams that need the widest model choice
best_when: EU data residency is required, or you want European AI infrastructure on principle. Also good for cost-efficient agent workloads.
avoid_when: You need frontier model performance (Claude Opus, GPT-4o) or complex multi-agent orchestration features.

auth.notes: Simple API key authentication compatible with OpenAI SDK format. Clean, well-documented auth.

pricing.paid_tiers: Per-token pricing: Mistral Small ~$0.20/M tokens, Mistral Large ~$2/M tokens. Agents add orchestration overhead cost.
pricing.notes: Very competitive pricing vs GPT-4 / Claude for similar capability tier. EU pricing in euros available.

performance.rate_limits: Varies by model tier; documented at docs.mistral.ai

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Clear, structured error responses. Token limit, rate limit, and authentication errors are well-documented. Tool call errors include what went wrong.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: LLM responses are non-deterministic. No idempotency for agent runs.
known_agent_gotchas: No MCP server — agents must use REST or Mistral SDK directly
Agent sessions are not persisted across API calls — memory is stateless
Native code execution is sandboxed but has runtime limits
Web search tool has coverage limitations vs. Google/Bing
Function calling schema is OpenAI-compatible but has some differences
EU-only data residency is a strength but limits latency for non-EU users
agent_readiness.security_notes: EU cloud infrastructure with GDPR compliance built in. SOC2 Type II, ISO 27001. Good enterprise compliance posture.
